Investor Anatomy
Copper Composite Pricing Model
Home
Copper Composite Pricing Signal
The Copper Composite Pricing Signal aggregates multiple copper equity pricing signals into a coherent view of copper market pricing dynamics.

Copper Pricing Regime — Qualitative Interpretation

Executive Summary

Copper pricing behaviour is currently defined by a broad and established deterioration among established producers (1)(2). Price action across the cohort shows consistent monthly declines exceeding 11%, accompanied by technical breakdowns below 50-day moving averages (3). High realised volatility persists as pricing positions migrate toward the middle of their respective 52-week ranges (1). The regime is currently characterised by synchronous downward movement among major operating assets, while other segments of the complex lack clear directional impetus. Internal consistency remains high within the producer group, though participation has not yet broadened to a universal cross-cohort alignment.

Producers

Directionalitydeteriorating
Breadthbroad
Persistenceestablished
Dispersionlow

Developers

Directionalitymixed
Breadthnarrow
Persistencefading
Dispersionmoderate

Processors / Fabricators

Directionalitymixed
Breadthnarrow
Persistencefading
Dispersionmoderate

Cross-Cohort Alignment

The copper complex is currently partially aligned, with isolated weakness concentrated in the producer cohort (1)(2). Producers are functioning as the leading cohort for the observed downward shift in pricing momentum. Participation has not yet broadened to developers or processors, resulting in an uneven movement across the wider copper industry. While the primary production assets exhibit synchronous technical breakdowns, the lack of directional confirmation from other segments indicates a fragmented rather than universal regime shift (3).

Inputs
Copper Wire Pricing Signal

Model Signal (Daily)

The daily signal is constructed from trend and momentum: uptrend + positive momentum tends to generate Add bias; downtrend + negative momentum tends to generate Reduce bias.

Company Profile

  • Name: Freeport-McMoRan Inc.
  • Sector: Basic Materials
  • Industry: Copper
  • Country: United States
  • Currency: USD

Description:

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. engages in the mining of mineral properties in North America, South America, and Indonesia. It primarily explores for copper, gold, molybdenum, silver, and other metals. The company's assets include the Grasberg minerals district in Indonesia; Morenci, Bagdad, Safford, Sierrita, and Miami in Arizona; Chino and Tyrone in New Mexico; and Henderson and Climax in Colorado, North America, as well as Cerro Verde in Peru and El Abra in Chile. Freeport-McMoRan Inc. was formerly known as Freeport-McMoRan Copper & Gold Inc. and changed its name to Freeport-McMoRan Inc. in July 2014. The company was incorporated in 1987 and is headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona.

Company Profile

  • Name: Sandfire Resources Limited
  • Sector: Basic Materials
  • Industry: Copper
  • Country: Australia
  • Currency: AUD

Description:

Sandfire Resources Limited, a mining company, explores for, evaluates, and develops mineral tenements and projects. It primarily explores for copper, gold, silver, lead, and zinc deposits. The company was incorporated in 2003 and is based in West Perth, Australia.
